decisions.title: Concerns about performance identified for Horizon Dashboard.
decisions.detail: There are identified concerns regarding the performance of the Horizon Dashboard project.

evidence.quote: there are some concerns about performance.

decisions.title: Consideration to use a better database at some point.
decisions.detail: There was a suggestion that a better database should probably be used for the project at some future point.

evidence.quote: Someone mentioned we should probably use a better database at some point.

decisions.title: UI aesthetic improvement required.
decisions.detail: The User Interface (UI) of the dashboard needs to be improved visually to look nicer.

evidence.quote: The UI needs to look nicer.

decisions.title: Current UI design is not satisfactory.
decisions.detail: The team agreed that the current design of the UI is not considered great.

evidence.quote: We all agreed the current design isn't great.

decisions.title: Timelines remain undecided.
decisions.detail: Although there was a discussion about timelines, no firm decisions were made.

evidence.quote: There was a discussion about timelines but nothing was firmly decided.

decisions.title: Potential future integration with CRM system.
decisions.detail: Integration with the CRM system might be necessary eventually, but the timing and method are currently unknown.

evidence.quote: We might need to integrate with the CRM system eventually, but we're not sure when or how.

decisions.title: Budget identified as a concern.
decisions.detail: Budget was briefly mentioned and is a concern, though no specific numbers or details were shared.

evidence.quote: Budget was mentioned briefly — it's a concern but no numbers were shared.

decisions.title: Testing thoroughness requires improvement.
decisions.detail: The team lead suggested that testing processes should be more thorough going forward.

evidence.quote: The team lead hinted that testing should be more thorough.

decisions.title: Better documentation recommended.
decisions.detail: It was suggested that documentation practices should be improved moving forward.

evidence.quote: We should probably document things better going forward.

implementation_scope: Implement performance optimizations for the Horizon Dashboard system to address identified concerns.
Redesign and re-implement User Interface (UI) components of the Horizon Dashboard to improve aesthetics.
Improve thoroughness of testing processes, including potential enhancements to automated testing frameworks and coverage.
Improve documentation practices and standards for existing and new code.

constraints.text: Address identified performance concerns for the Horizon Dashboard.
Project budget is a concern, with specific numbers and details pending.

open_technical_questions: Should a better database be selected and implemented for the Horizon Dashboard? (inferred_needs_confirmation from D002)
What is the scope, timing, and technical method for integrating with the CRM system? (inferred_needs_confirmation from D006)

acceptance_criteria: Given a user navigates to the Horizon Dashboard, When the UI is displayed, Then all visual elements (e.g., layout, typography, colors, iconography, component styling) adhere to the newly defined design specifications and visual guidelines.

Given a user interacts with the Horizon Dashboard UI, When performing actions such as clicking buttons or navigating pages, Then all interactive elements visually respond according to the approved design specifications.
test_scenarios: Verify the overall aesthetic appearance of the Horizon Dashboard UI across all main pages and modules.
Verify that all UI components (e.g., forms, tables, charts, buttons, navigation menus) reflect the new visual design consistently.
Validate that the dashboard UI maintains its new aesthetic and layout integrity when viewed on various screen sizes and resolutions (e.g., desktop, tablet, mobile).
edge_cases: Test UI rendering when data fields contain maximum allowable characters to ensure layout is not broken.
Test UI rendering when lists or tables contain a very large number of items to ensure performance and visual integrity.
Test UI rendering when there is no data to display in a component, ensuring a graceful display (e.g., 'No data available' message) that aligns with the new aesthetic.
unresolved_behaviors: What are the specific performance metrics (e.g., page load times, response times) and acceptable thresholds for the Horizon Dashboard to address the identified performance concerns? (Owned by PM/QA)
What are the specific 'nicer' UI design standards, mockups, or design system specifications that the dashboard UI needs to adhere to for aesthetic improvement? (Owned by PM/QA)
What specific criteria will define 'thorough' testing for this project (e.g., code coverage targets, defect escape rate goals, types of tests required)? (Owned by PM/QA)
What specific product documentation needs to be improved/created (e.g., user guides for the UI changes) and what are the acceptance criteria for its quality and completeness? (Owned by PM/QA)

decision_history: It was confirmed that the User Interface (UI) of the dashboard needs aesthetic improvement.
The team agreed that the current UI design is not satisfactory and requires rework.
dependencies: Potential future integration with the CRM system has been identified, but the timing and method are currently unknown (inferred dependency, needs clarification to confirm).
risks: Concerns about performance have been identified for the Horizon Dashboard project (likelihood: confirmed, impact: potentially high on user experience and adoption).
Budget has been identified as a concern, though no specific numbers or details were shared (likelihood: confirmed, impact: unknown but potentially high on scope and timeline).
alignment_gaps: Timelines for the project remain undecided despite discussion, indicating a lack of clear agreement or commitment on planning.
A suggestion to use a better database at some point was made but not confirmed, indicating a potential unaddressed technical consideration that could impact future performance and stability.
The need for more thorough testing was suggested but not firmly decided, implying potential differing expectations on quality assurance standards.
Improved documentation practices were recommended but not confirmed, suggesting a potential gap in agreement on project engineering standards.
open_questions: What are the confirmed timelines and deadlines for the Horizon Dashboard project? (Ownership: PM, Impact: High - critical for all planning and commitments).

Is the potential integration with the CRM system a confirmed requirement? If so, what is the agreed scope, method, and timeline for this cross-system dependency? (Ownership: PM, Impact: High - could significantly alter project scope and effort).
What are the specific requirements and implementation plan for improving testing thoroughness within the project? (Ownership: PM, Impact: Medium - affects quality assurance and release confidence).

What are the specific requirements and an implementation plan for improving documentation practices moving forward? (Ownership: PM, Impact: Medium - affects maintainability, knowledge transfer, and onboarding).

Is the suggestion to use a better database a confirmed architectural direction for the project, and if so, what is the timeline for evaluation and implementation, especially in light of current performance concerns? (Ownership: PM, Impact: Medium - relates to core performance risk and future scalability).

system_requirements: The Horizon Dashboard system SHALL demonstrate improved performance to address identified concerns (D001).
The Horizon Dashboard UI SHALL be aesthetically improved to provide a more satisfactory user experience (D003, D004).
data_flows: The Horizon Dashboard system SHALL be capable of exchanging data with the CRM system (inferred — needs confirmation, D006).
interface_contracts: A formal interface contract SHALL be defined to specify data exchange mechanisms, protocols, and data models for potential integration between the Horizon Dashboard and the CRM system (inferred — needs confirmation, D006).

constraints.text: The Horizon Dashboard system currently experiences identified performance concerns.
The project development is subject to budget limitations.
The current database is considered inadequate for future project needs, necessitating consideration of a superior alternative.

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific performance metrics and target thresholds for the Horizon Dashboard system that define 'improved performance'?
What specific database technology or approach will be selected to address the identified performance and scalability concerns, and what is the scope and timeline for its implementation?
What data entities, attributes, and operations are required for the integration with the CRM system, and what are the functional requirements driving this integration?

stakeholder.business_summary: This meeting focused on the Horizon Dashboard project, highlighting concerns about its current performance and the need for significant improvements to its user interface and overall quality. Key issues like budget and project timelines remain open, requiring executive input to move forward.
key_impact: Users may experience slower performance with the Horizon Dashboard, potentially affecting productivity and satisfaction.
The dashboard's appearance (user interface) needs a complete overhaul, which will require design and development resources.
Uncertainty around project timelines and budget could delay improvements and increase overall costs if not addressed promptly.
Future integration with the CRM system is a possibility, which could streamline operations but requires careful planning.
decisions_needing_alignment: Confirm if a significant upgrade to the underlying database technology is required for better performance.
Approve the increased thoroughness in testing processes to ensure higher quality.
Approve the recommendation for improving documentation practices.
Confirm the necessity and timing of integrating the dashboard with our CRM system.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the target timelines for addressing the performance and user interface issues?
What is the available budget for the necessary improvements and potential system upgrades?
When and how should the Horizon Dashboard integrate with our CRM system to maximize business value?

issues.description: The claim 'A formal interface contract SHALL be defined...' overstates the certainty and scope of decision D006. D006 only indicates 'Potential future integration with CRM system' with unknown timing and method, not a confirmed requirement for a formal contract.
issues.suggestion: Rephrase to reflect the 'inferred_needs_confirmation' status of D006 and avoid stating the necessity of a formal contract as a firm requirement. It should be presented as a potential architectural task pending confirmation of the integration.

issues.description: The constraint 'The current database is considered inadequate for future project needs, necessitating consideration of a superior alternative' overstates the confidence of decision D002. D002 is an 'inferred_needs_confirmation' suggestion that a better database 'should probably be used at some point', not a confirmed inadequacy or necessity.
issues.suggestion: Rephrase to align with the 'inferred_needs_confirmation' status of D002. For example, 'A suggestion was made to consider a better database for future project needs, which is currently an inferred constraint needing confirmation.'

review.reviewer_notes: Two critical issues were identified in the SA View related to overstating the certainty and scope of decisions D002 and D006. These should be corrected to accurately reflect the 'inferred_needs_confirmation' status of the original decisions.
